title: Coronavirus: Juliette Kayyem on Stages of Crisis Management
summary: Juliette Kayyem, Faculty Chair of the Belfer Center’s Security and Global Health Project, has been providing real-time perspective on the coronavirus on Twitter.
body: Juliette Kayyem, Faculty Chair of the Belfer Center’s Security and Global Health Project, has been providing real-time perspective on the coronavirus on Twitter. Read her recent comments below. The latest from Belfer See more THREAD. Updated crisis management chart to get our bearings for years ahead. New addition: stop talking about recovery. It’s misleading. It won’t come until vaccine distribution. We are entering a totally unprecedented stage, I’ll call it “adaptive recovery.” #coronavirus 1/ pic.twitter.com/MzOacgj1Mj — Juliette Kayyem (@juliettekayyem) April 9, 2020 Click here to view a larger version of Kayyem's chart. I advise a lot of mayors, govs and biz leaders to help them give context to these strange times. Sharing this now. Virus is new; crisis management is not. We had in past always talked about recovery as getting back to normal. That’s not what we are doing here. 2/ — Juliette Kayyem (@juliettekayyem) April 9, 2020 We will (where are the kits!) move past social distancing to a phase that is best understood as managing to live with and adapt to the virus. It isn’t normal and it may be years. But we will have choices besides staying at home: better testing/tracking, treatments, etc. 3/ — Juliette Kayyem (@juliettekayyem) April 9, 2020 It will not be normal until vaccine and 18 months is optimistic for identification and distribution. But it will come. Until then, it will be a recovery of sorts but one where everyday we will need to take adaptive measures to avoid more spread. 4/ — Juliette Kayyem (@juliettekayyem) April 9, 2020 To say we have only a small sense of what this will feel like is understatement. A CEO has to set a standard for a office closing should an employee test positive; a mayor for a school; etc. As citizens, we will need to adapt on daily basis. It is a different kind of recovery. 5/ — Juliette Kayyem (@juliettekayyem) April 9, 2020 This is update to previous chart circulated a few weeks back. It held up ok but my thinking has changed. The only true recovery is vaccine. The idea of it being normal anytime soon is fiction from WH. Hope this helps. Thanks @BloombergCities 6/ — Juliette Kayyem (@juliettekayyem) April 9, 2020 And finally, more personally, from @WakeDivDean Jonathan Walton, getting our footing for all this is to live in the “now normal” rather than an elusive search for “new normal.” We find our normal now because tomorrow may be different. Now. Virus sets the rest of the schedule 7/7 — Juliette Kayyem (@juliettekayyem) April 9, 2020 Recommended citation Kayyem, Juliette. “Coronavirus: Juliette Kayyem on Stages of Crisis Management.” Belfer Center for Science and International Affairs, Harvard Kennedy School , April 9, 2020 Copy to Clipboard Coronavirus: Juliette Kayyem on Stages of Crisis Management View Citation Print Coronavirus Global Health Public Health Recommended citation Kayyem, Juliette. “Coronavirus: Juliette Kayyem on Stages of Crisis Management.” Belfer Center for Science and International Affairs, Harvard Kennedy School , April 9, 2020 Copy to Clipboard Author Juliette Kayyem Request Interview More From This Expert Securing the World’s Biggest Moments: Major Strategies in Megaevent Safety Planning Managing Supply And Value Chains In A New Era Of Global Trade: The Promise Of Federated Learning Up Next Photo Credit Toggle Associated Press - Daniel Kozin Blog Post H3N2 “Subclade K” Is Spreading. Here’s What It Means for Severity, Vaccines, and Treatment Dec. 23, 2025 by Syra Madad From Emerging Technology, Scientific Advancement, and Global Policy Photo Credit Toggle ASSOCIATED PRESS/Mark J. _list.source: Belfer Center for Science and International Affairs, Harvard Kennedy School

result.main_content_en: Juliette Kayyem, Faculty Chair of the Belfer Center's Security and Global Health Project, provides a framework for managing the COVID-19 crisis, moving beyond traditional recovery models. She argues that the concept of returning to normal is misleading until a vaccine is widely distributed, which may take years. Instead, she introduces the stage of 'adaptive recovery,' where societies must learn to live with the virus through ongoing mitigation measures. This phase involves implementing better testing, contact tracing, and treatments while making daily adaptations to prevent spread. Kayyem emphasizes that crisis management fundamentals remain applicable even for a novel virus, advising leaders to prepare for long-term operational adjustments in workplaces, schools, and communities rather than expecting a swift return to pre-pandemic conditions.
result.main_content_zh: 贝尔弗中心安全与全球健康项目主席朱丽叶·凯耶姆为管理新冠疫情提供了一个框架，超越了传统的恢复模式。她认为，在疫苗广泛分发之前（这可能需数年时间），回归正常的想法是具有误导性的。相反，她提出了'适应性恢复'阶段，社会必须通过持续的缓解措施学会与病毒共存。这一阶段涉及实施更好的检测、接触者追踪和治疗方案，同时进行日常调整以防止传播。凯耶姆强调，即使面对新型病毒，危机管理的基本原则仍然适用，她建议领导者为工作场所、学校和社区的长期运营调整做好准备，而不是期望迅速恢复到疫情前的状态。
